SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.state

Class SQLiteStateStore

java.lang.Object
edu.yu.cs.com3810.state.SQLiteStateStore
All Implemented Interfaces:
StateStore
public class SQLiteStateStore extends Object implements StateStore
SQLite-backed implementation of StateStore.

Stores all coordinator state — users, devices, device actions, and automation rules — in a single SQLite database file. SQLite requires no server process; the database lives as a file on the coordinator's local disk.

Schema

 users
   user_id    TEXT PK
   username   TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL
   created_at TEXT NOT NULL          (ISO-8601)

 devices
   device_id  TEXT PK
   user_id    TEXT FK → users
   name       TEXT NOT NULL
   type       TEXT NOT NULL          (DeviceType enum name)
   online     INTEGER NOT NULL       (0 = offline, 1 = online)
   created_at TEXT NOT NULL

 device_actions
   device_id  TEXT FK → devices (CASCADE DELETE)
   action     TEXT NOT NULL          (DeviceAction enum name)
   PK (device_id, action)

 automation_rules
   rule_id             TEXT PK
   user_id             TEXT FK → users
   rule_name           TEXT NOT NULL
   trigger_device_type TEXT NOT NULL
   trigger_event_type  TEXT NOT NULL
   trigger_device_id   TEXT          (nullable — null = wildcard)
   target_device_id    TEXT NOT NULL FK → devices
   target_action       TEXT NOT NULL
   active              INTEGER NOT NULL (0/1)
   created_at          TEXT NOT NULL

Thread safety

All public methods are synchronized on this instance. SQLite in WAL mode supports concurrent reads but only one writer at a time — the synchronized methods ensure writes are serialised at the Java level, which is sufficient for the coordinator's throughput.

Transactions

Multi-statement operations (e.g. saving a device + its actions) are wrapped in explicit transactions so a crash mid-write never leaves the database in a partially updated state.

  • Constructor Details

    • SQLiteStateStore

      public SQLiteStateStore(String dbPath)
      Constructs a SQLiteStateStore.
      Parameters:
      dbPath - path to the SQLite database file, e.g. "data/coordinator.db". The file is created if it does not exist.

  • Method Details

    • init

      public void init()
      Initialises the store — creates tables if they do not exist, opens connection pools, etc. Called once during component startup before any other method is invoked.

      Opens the SQLite connection, enables WAL mode for better concurrent read performance, enables foreign key enforcement (off by default in SQLite), and creates all tables if they do not exist.

      Specified by:
      init in interface StateStore

    • close

      public void close()
      Closes the store cleanly — flushes pending writes, closes connections. Called during component shutdown.
      Specified by:
      close in interface StateStore

    • saveUser

      public void saveUser(User user)
      Persists a new user. Throws if a user with the same ID or username already exists.
      Specified by:
      saveUser in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      user - the user to save; must not be null

    • getUserById

      public Optional<User> getUserById(String userId)
      Returns the user with the given ID, or Optional.empty() if not found.
      Specified by:
      getUserById in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      userId - the ID to look up
      Returns:
      the matching user, or empty

    • getUserByUsername

      public Optional<User> getUserByUsername(String username)
      Returns the user with the given username, or Optional.empty() if not found. Used during login / request authentication.
      Specified by:
      getUserByUsername in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      username - the username to look up
      Returns:
      the matching user, or empty

    • getAllUsers

      public List<User> getAllUsers()
      Returns all users registered in the system.
      Specified by:
      getAllUsers in interface StateStore
      Returns:
      list of all users; empty list if none exist

    • deleteUser

      public void deleteUser(String userId)
      Deletes a user and cascades — removes all devices and rules owned by that user.

      Cascades via foreign key constraints: deletes devices (and their actions) and rules owned by this user.

      Specified by:
      deleteUser in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      userId - the ID of the user to delete

    • saveDevice

      public void saveDevice(Device device)
      Persists a newly registered device and its supported actions. If a device with the same ID already exists, updates it (upsert).

      Uses a transaction to insert/replace the device row and re-insert all of its action rows atomically. Existing action rows are deleted first to handle the case where a device re-registers with a different action set.

      Specified by:
      saveDevice in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      device - the device to save; must not be null

    • claimDevice

      public void claimDevice(ClaimDeviceRequest claimRequest)
      Description copied from interface: StateStore
      Creates a pending device record before the physical device has connected. Called when a user claims a device in the app by providing its ID, name, and type. The record is stored with online=false and no actions (capabilities are unknown until the device connects and sends its RegistrationPayload).

      If a record for this device ID already exists (device connected before the user claimed it), updates the existing record with the userId and name rather than inserting a duplicate.

      Specified by:
      claimDevice in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      claimRequest - the claim request containing user ID, device ID, name, and type

    • getDeviceById

      public Optional<Device> getDeviceById(String deviceId)
      Returns the device with the given ID, or Optional.empty() if not found.
      Specified by:
      getDeviceById in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the device ID to look up
      Returns:
      the matching device, or empty

    • getDevicesByUser

      public List<Device> getDevicesByUser(String userId)
      Returns all devices registered by the given user.
      Specified by:
      getDevicesByUser in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      userId - the user whose devices to retrieve
      Returns:
      list of devices owned by this user; empty list if none

    • getAllDevices

      public List<Device> getAllDevices()
      Returns all devices registered in the system across all users. Used by the coordinator on startup to rebuild its in-memory device registry.
      Specified by:
      getAllDevices in interface StateStore
      Returns:
      list of all devices; empty list if none

    • updateDeviceOnlineStatus

      public void updateDeviceOnlineStatus(String deviceId, boolean online)
      Updates the online/offline status of a device. Called by the coordinator's heartbeat monitor when a device's liveness changes — does not affect any other device fields.
      Specified by:
      updateDeviceOnlineStatus in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device to update
      online - true to mark online, false to mark offline

    • updateDeviceState

      public void updateDeviceState(String deviceId, Map<String,String> state)
      Updates the persistent state for a device.
      Specified by:
      updateDeviceState in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device to update
      state - a map of key-value pairs representing the device's state

    • deleteDevice

      public void deleteDevice(String deviceId)
      Removes a device and all its supported actions from the store.

      The ON DELETE CASCADE on device_actions removes the device's action rows automatically.

      Specified by:
      deleteDevice in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device to delete

    • saveRule

      public void saveRule(AutomationRule rule)
      Persists a new automation rule. If a rule with the same ID already exists, updates it (upsert).
      Specified by:
      saveRule in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      rule - the rule to save; must not be null

    • getRuleById

      public Optional<AutomationRule> getRuleById(String ruleId)
      Returns the automation rule with the given ID, or Optional.empty() if not found.
      Specified by:
      getRuleById in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the rule ID to look up
      Returns:
      the matching rule, or empty

    • getRulesByUser

      public List<AutomationRule> getRulesByUser(String userId)
      Returns all automation rules owned by the given user. Includes both active and inactive rules.
      Specified by:
      getRulesByUser in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      userId - the user whose rules to retrieve
      Returns:
      list of rules owned by this user; empty list if none

    • getAllActiveRules

      public List<AutomationRule> getAllActiveRules()
      Returns all active automation rules across all users. Used by the Automation Hub on startup to load its working rule set without waiting for the coordinator to replay rules.
      Specified by:
      getAllActiveRules in interface StateStore
      Returns:
      list of all active rules; empty list if none

    • getAllRules

      public List<AutomationRule> getAllRules()
      Returns all automation rules across all users. Used by the Automation Hub on startup to load its working rule set without waiting for the coordinator to replay rules.
      Specified by:
      getAllRules in interface StateStore
      Returns:
      list of all active rules; empty list if none

    • setRuleActive

      public void setRuleActive(String ruleId, boolean active)
      Updates the active/inactive flag of an existing rule. Called by toggle operations — does not affect any other rule fields.
      Specified by:
      setRuleActive in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to update
      active - true to activate, false to deactivate

    • deleteRule

      public void deleteRule(String ruleId)
      Removes an automation rule from the store.
      Specified by:
      deleteRule in interface StateStore
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to delete

    • loadStateForDevice

      public Map<String,String> loadStateForDevice(String deviceId) throws SQLException
      Specified by:
      loadStateForDevice in interface StateStore
      Throws:
      SQLException
